title: goal-halt-report
description: Schema for the goal-run harness halt-report object (issue #874, plan step 1, AC2). One object per halt: posted as a durable `<!-- goal-halt-report-{ID} -->` GitHub issue comment by the schema-validating emitter in .github/scripts/lib/goal-run-halt-core.ps1 (Invoke-GoalRunHaltEmit), which refuses to post any object that fails this schema. Closed schema (additionalProperties: false) so the emitter cannot silently pass through an unnamed field -- this is part of the transcript-content barrier (#874 plan step 1 item 3): every field an executor or the harness can populate here is named and typed by this file. This schema defines the emit primitive's shape only; it does NOT encode halt-reason precedence (a later #874 step decides which halt_reason wins when multiple conditions are true at once).

schema_version.description: The accepted schema_version set. Callers read this enum rather than hard-coding accepted versions.

issue.description: The GitHub issue number the goal-run harness invocation belongs to.

halt_reason.description: The five-value halt-condition enum, reused verbatim from skills/plan-authoring/schemas/goal-contract.schema.json's halt_conditions items enum (872-D2) -- this is the single literal on this report identifying WHY the run halted.

target_ref.description: Identifier of the goal-contract target (targets[].id or ac_ref) this halt relates to, or null when the halt is not tied to a single target (e.g. budget-exhausted or chain-stage-failure may have no single target).

plan_remediation.description: Free-text guidance on how the plan or a human should respond to this halt. Executor/harness-authored, not raw transcript passthrough; still inert-rendered (marker literals neutralized) by the emitter before posting.

evidence.description: Evidence strings supporting the halt claim. Each item is inert-rendered by the emitter (marker-literal delimiters stripped) before posting, and any transcript-derived string reaching this array must already have passed through the allow-list extractor and secret-redaction pass in goal-run-transcript-core.ps1 -- this schema cannot itself enforce that upstream discipline, only the closed shape.

recommended_next_owner.description: Who or what should act on this halt next (e.g. a role name, 'maintainer', 'Code-Conductor').

arm.description: Which execution arm produced this halt.

stage.description: Which pipeline stage was in progress when the halt occurred.

claim_provenance.description: Who is asserting this halt claim -- the executor's own self-report, an independent validator, or the harness itself (e.g. a budget-ceiling trip the harness observed directly).

budget_snapshot.description: Typed/numeric budget fields only (transcript-content barrier: no free-text transcript passthrough here). Which fields are populated depends on halt_reason -- e.g. budget-exhausted typically populates tokens_used/tokens_budget, a wall-clock halt populates the wall_clock_* pair -- so no field is individually required.

tokens_budget.description: Token ceiling, or an advisory string, mirroring goal-contract.schema.json's budget.tokens flexibility (#871 finding).
